product_overview: PixelGrab is a public, no-login web app that extracts dominant color palettes from user-uploaded images and provides copy/export tools for designers and developers. Users upload an image, review extracted swatches in HEX/RGB/HSL, copy values, and export snippets as CSS, JSON, or Tailwind config.
core_goals: Quickly extract dominant colors from a single image and view them as reusable color swatches.
Switch between HEX, RGB, and HSL representations of extracted colors.
Copy individual color values or copy all values for easy reuse.
Export the generated palette as CSS variables, JSON tokens, or a Tailwind config snippet and download or copy the export.

features.name: Landing page navigation
features.description: Introduce PixelGrab and let users discover and open the palette extraction tool.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Read the PixelGrab product description and feature cards -> Decide to open the palette tool -> Navigate to /palette -> See the palette upload area
Navigate to / -> Read product description -> Choose Try it now to open the palette tool -> Navigate to /palette -> See the upload dropzone and supported file guidance

features.name: Image upload by click or drag and drop
features.description: Upload a single PNG, JPEG, GIF, or WebP image up to 10 MB using click-to-browse or drag-and-drop, with visual feedback for drag-active and rejected files.
user_flows: Navigate to /palette -> Upload a valid PNG, JPEG, GIF, or WebP under 10 MB via browse -> See drag-active messaging only while dragging (if applicable) -> See loading skeleton while extraction runs
Navigate to /palette -> Drag and drop a valid image under 10 MB onto the dropzone -> See drag-active messaging during drag -> See loading skeleton while extraction runs
Navigate to /palette -> Attempt to upload a non-image file -> See rejected-file messaging for unsupported file type
Navigate to /palette -> Attempt to upload an image larger than 10 MB -> See rejected-file messaging for oversized file

features.name: Palette extraction and display
features.description: Extract distinct dominant colors from the uploaded image and present the source image and a grid of swatches with color values and controls to clear or re-upload.
user_flows: Navigate to /palette -> Upload a valid image under 10 MB -> See loading skeleton while extraction runs -> See the uploaded source image displayed -> See extracted palette swatches and their color values

Navigate to /palette -> Upload a very small or single-color image -> See loading skeleton while extraction runs -> See either a grid with the identified dominant color(s) or an empty state if no dominant colors are identified
Navigate to /palette -> Upload a corrupted or broken image file -> See a visible error state indicating the image could not be processed
Navigate to /palette -> After viewing extracted swatches, choose Upload new image -> Return to an empty upload state ready for a new file

features.name: Color format switching and copy actions
features.description: Allow users to switch displayed color values between HEX, RGB, and HSL and copy individual values or all values with user feedback.
user_flows: Navigate to /palette -> Upload a valid image -> View extracted palette swatches -> Switch output format to RGB -> See all displayed color values update to RGB
Navigate to /palette -> Upload a valid image -> View extracted palette swatches -> Click a color value to copy it -> See success toast feedback confirming the value was copied
Navigate to /palette -> Upload a valid image -> View extracted palette swatches -> Click Copy All -> See success toast feedback confirming all displayed values were copied

features.name: Palette export
features.description: Generate export snippets from the extracted palette as CSS variables, JSON tokens, or a Tailwind config snippet, and allow copying or downloading the snippet.
user_flows: Navigate to /palette -> Upload a valid image -> View extracted palette swatches -> Open the Export CSS modal -> Copy the CSS variables snippet from the modal -> Close the modal
Navigate to /palette -> Upload a valid image -> View extracted palette swatches -> Open the Export JSON modal -> Copy the JSON tokens snippet from the modal -> Download the JSON file from the modal
Navigate to /palette -> Upload a valid image -> View extracted palette swatches -> Open the Export Tailwind modal -> Copy the Tailwind config snippet from the modal -> Close the modal

routes.description: Landing page introducing PixelGrab with CTA to the palette tool.
Main image upload, palette extraction, color copy, and export workflow.

features.name: Landing page navigation
features.description: Users can understand the product and navigate to the palette extraction tool.
user_interactions: View the PixelGrab product description and feature cards.
Click Open Palette Tool.
Click Try it now to open the palette tool.

features.name: Image upload by click or drag and drop
features.description: Users upload one PNG, JPEG, GIF, or WebP image up to 10 MB.
user_interactions: Open the palette page.
Click the dropzone to browse for an image.
Drag and drop an image onto the dropzone.
See drag-active messaging before releasing the file.
See rejected-file messaging for unsupported files or files over 10 MB.

features.name: Palette extraction and display
features.description: The app extracts distinct dominant colors from the uploaded image and displays a color grid.
user_interactions: Upload a valid image.
Wait for the loading skeleton while extraction runs.
View the uploaded source image.
View extracted palette swatches and color values.
Clear the image with Upload new image.
See an error state if palette extraction fails.
See an empty state if no dominant colors are identified.

features.name: Color format switching and copy actions
features.description: Users can switch color output format and copy individual or all generated color values.
user_interactions: Switch between HEX, RGB, and HSL output formats.
Click a color value to copy it.
Click Copy All to copy all displayed values.
See success or failure toast feedback for clipboard actions.

features.name: Palette export
features.description: Users can generate CSS variables, JSON tokens, or Tailwind config snippets from the palette.
user_interactions: Click Export CSS to open the CSS Variables modal.
Click Export JSON to open the JSON modal.
Click Export Tailwind to open the Tailwind Config modal.
Copy an export snippet from the modal.
Download an export file from the modal.
Close the export modal.

known_limitations.issue: Paste or clipboard image upload is not implemented.
known_limitations.impact: Users must use browse or drag-and-drop upload.

known_limitations.issue: The dropzone clear button only clears its local preview, while the parent page already moves to the extracted state after a file is accepted.
known_limitations.impact: The remove-preview button is not reachable in the normal current flow after upload.

known_limitations.issue: README tech stack section is outdated and mentions Svelte/JavaScript despite the app using Next.js, React, and TypeScript.
known_limitations.impact: Public project documentation can confuse reviewers.
